title: EU AI Act Risk Classification Questionnaire
description: Guided questionnaire to determine the EU AI Act risk category for an AI system, based on Annex III high-risk categories.

steps.title: AI System Type
steps.question: What type of AI system is this?

options.label: Prohibited AI practices
options.description: Social scoring, real-time biometric identification in public spaces (with exceptions), emotion recognition in workplace/education, manipulation techniques

options.label: General-purpose AI system
options.description: Foundation model or general-purpose AI model

options.label: Specific-purpose AI system
options.description: AI system designed for a specific use case

steps.title: General-Purpose AI Classification
steps.question: Does this GPAI model pose systemic risk (>10^25 FLOPs training compute)?

steps.title: Use Domain (Annex III)
steps.question: In which domain is this AI system used?

options.label: Biometrics
options.description: Remote biometric identification, emotion recognition, biometric categorisation

options.label: Critical infrastructure
options.description: Safety components of critical infrastructure (water, gas, heating, electricity, road traffic, supply of digital infrastructure)

options.label: Education and vocational training
options.description: Access to education, evaluation of learning outcomes, assessment of appropriate level of education, monitoring of prohibited behaviour during tests

options.label: Employment and workers management
options.description: Recruitment, selection, HR decisions, task allocation, performance monitoring

options.label: Essential private and public services
options.description: Creditworthiness assessment, insurance risk, emergency services dispatch, social benefits eligibility

options.label: Law enforcement
options.description: Individual risk assessment, polygraphs, evidence reliability, profiling, crime analytics

options.label: Migration, asylum and border control
options.description: Polygraphs, risk assessment, document authenticity verification, asylum application examination

options.label: Administration of justice and democratic processes
options.description: AI assisting judicial authorities in researching, interpreting facts and law, applying the law

options.label: General / Other
options.description: None of the above high-risk domains

steps.title: Transparency Obligations
steps.question: Does this AI system have transparency obligations?

options.label: Yes - interacts directly with natural persons
options.description: Chatbots, virtual assistants, customer service AI

options.label: Yes - generates synthetic content (text, images, audio, video)
Yes - emotion recognition or biometric categorisation (non-prohibited)

unacceptable.description: This AI system falls under prohibited AI practices (Article 5). It cannot be placed on the EU market.
unacceptable.next_steps: Consult legal counsel. The system must be redesigned or withdrawn.

high.description: This AI system is classified as high-risk under the EU AI Act (Annex III or Article 6). Full compliance requirements apply.
high.next_steps: Complete all Annex IV technical documentation, establish risk management system, ensure human oversight, conduct conformity assessment.

limited.description: This AI system has limited risk with transparency obligations (Article 50). Users must be informed about AI interaction.
limited.next_steps: Implement transparency measures: disclosure of AI nature, content marking as applicable.

minimal.description: This AI system is classified as minimal risk. No mandatory requirements, but voluntary codes of conduct are encouraged.
minimal.next_steps: Consider adopting voluntary codes of conduct and best practices.
